---
title: "Tasks 2 and 3"
author: "Bryan Sandor"
format: html
---
# Preamble
This is a temporary placeholder until I have access to the vectors from Task 1

```{r}
patient <- c("Subject_1", "Subject_2", "Subject_3")
pre_bp <- c(119, 132, 118)
post_bp <- c(132, 119, 127)
diff_bp <- pre_bp - post_bp
```


# Task 2: Basic Data Frame practice
This is continued from the previous example, wherein four vectors were used:
`patient`, `pre_bp`, `post_bp`, and `diff_bp`.

1. First we create a data frame object with the four columns corresponding to the
previously mentioned vectors:
```{r}
bd_df <- data.frame(patient, pre_bp, post_bp, diff_bp)
bd_df
```

2. Now we return just the rows where `diff_bp` is negative (indicating a decrease
in blood pressure).
```{r}
for(i in 1:length(bd_df$diff_bp)){
  if (bd_df$diff_bp[i] < 0) {
    print(bd_df[i, ])
  }
}
```


# Task 3: List practice
T
